steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says ferritic stainless steel (induction cookers) contains a higher percentage of chromium (at least 10.5%) than austenitic stainless steel. The 200 series (austenitic) is used for dishwashers; the 300 series (austenitic) for storage containers/sinks. Both austenitic subtypes have less chromium than ferritic. The question asks which inference follows logically.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — sinks will have similar magnetic properties to stainless steel used to manufacture induction cookers.: Sinks use austenitic (300 series), which lacks the high iron concentration responsible for magnetic properties; ferritic is magnetic, austenitic is not. ✗
  • Choice B — storage containers will have a concentration of chromium lower than 10.5%, while stainless steel used to manufacture dishwashers will not.: Both storage containers (300 series) and dishwashers (200 series) are austenitic; the passage distinguishes them by nickel content and use, not by chromium content, so this chromium-threshold contrast is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice C — induction cookers will have a face-centered cubic crystalline structure.: Induction cookers use ferritic stainless steel; the face-centered cubic structure belongs to austenitic, not ferritic. ✗
  • Choice D — dishwashers will have a lower percentage of chromium in its composition than stainless steel used for induction cookers will.: Dishwashers use austenitic (200 series), which has less chromium than ferritic (induction cookers). This follows directly from the passage. ✓
That is the correct answer.
